questions.prompt: You have a research feature that needs to plan, run several sub-research queries, and synthesize findings — with the sub-queries depending on the plan. Which pattern fits?

choices.text: Supervisor — a manager agent orchestrates specialist sub-agents as tools. The supervisor reasons about the plan, calls specialists for sub-queries, then composes the final synthesis. Fits tasks that decompose into specialized work but require holistic integration at the end.
Hand-off — pass the user from one agent to another.

questions.explanation: Supervisor is the canonical pattern for decompose-then-synthesize work. Routing fits clear category separation (this question = support, that one = billing). Hand-off fits conversational personas. Supervisor fits when one brain needs to coordinate multiple specialists and produce a unified result.

questions.prompt: What should flow between agents to keep multi-agent systems efficient?

choices.text: Structured handoff payloads — a schema-defined object with `{ task, context, constraints }` — when possible. Falls back to summaries for conversational agents. Full history is the lazy default; it's expensive and confuses specialists with irrelevant context.
No state — each agent starts fresh.

questions.explanation: Structured payloads are the cleanest pattern: the supervisor packages exactly what the specialist needs; the specialist returns exactly what the supervisor needs to continue. Cheaper than full history, more focused than summaries. Use summaries only when conversational continuity matters.
